content: Initial evaluation: high-resolution thyroid ultrasound characterizes nodules using TI-RADS or ATA patterns (hypoechogenicity, irregular margins, microcalcifications, taller-than-wide shape suggest malignancy). Fine-needle aspiration (FNA) biopsy indicated for nodules ≥1cm with suspicious features, or ≥1.5-2cm with intermediate suspicion. Bethesda System classifies FNA results: I (nondiagnostic), II (benign), III (atypia/follicular lesion of undetermined significance), IV (follicular neoplasm), V (suspicious for malignancy), VI (malignant). Molecular testing (Afirma, ThyroSeq) aids indeterminate nodules. Serum thyroglobulin for post-treatment surveillance of differentiated thyroid cancer. Calcitonin and CEA for medullary thyroid cancer diagnosis/monitoring. RET mutation testing for all MTC patients. Staging uses TNM system with age cutoff at 55 years for differentiated thyroid cancer.
